Trigger: Any phase that interprets IdealThread, CutSpec.full, Schur recursion, DtN boundary matrices, or string-like analogies.
Required input: Code references for IdealThread singleton carrier/family/coarsen lemmas; CutSpec.full carrier theorem; current generated boundary source; planned consumer shape.
Required updates: Record whether the phase consumes thread-scalar route, full-boundary route, or generated intermediate route. Mark analogical string language comparison-only unless a theorem gives a formal CNNA object.
Exit rule: No full matrix/operator, discriminant, spectrum, or physical interpretation may be promoted from the thread route unless the route and lifting theorem are explicitly present and code-checked.

Trigger: Whenever a phase introduces or consumes dynamics, spectra, operator algebra, geometry, arithmetic, stochasticity, open-system language, unitarity, AQFT/physics interpretation, or environment/complement effects.
Required input: POST1/LEG35/GNG22; source-object chain from IDEAL_infty formal background to subsystem/cut/approximant, boundary, regime, complement families and Schur/DtN or later certified bridge.
Required updates: Update object proof dossier, scratchpad source_to_cnna_mapping, dependency_depth_audit, no-go guardrails, and consumer-contract direction. Mark comparison-only interpretations explicitly and prohibit them from supplying definitions.
Exit rule: A phase may close only if non-subsystem structures are shown as derived consequences or comparison-only interpretations. Any primitive import of dynamics/physics/arithmetic/geometry/environment forces a backjump to the natural subsystem/complement generating layer.

Trigger: Before preparing Phase 3.6 or Phase 4.1, or any wrapper around BoundaryOperatorProfile / ExecBoundaryMatrix.
Required input: BoundaryOperatorProfile shape; Operational/ExecComplex consumer needs; import direction; generator policy.
Required updates: Fill consumer_contract_direction. Phase 3.6 records the exported shape; Phase 4.1 records how it consumes the shape without reconstructing Schur/DtN internals.
Exit rule: Phase 3 may not import Phase 4. Phase 4 may not duplicate generator/Schur/DtN logic; it consumes the Phase-3 profile contract.

Trigger: A phase attempts to relate Cayley-Dickson/Hurwitz algebra to arithmetic discriminant, CM order, norm, or transfermatrix data through Schur complementation.
Required input: Closed S05/BR06 horizontal boundary route, closed O05/BR07 vertical arithmetic-shadow route, Transfermatrix objects TM0-TM2, CD7 Hurwitz boundary, and explicit module manifest for the bridge.
Required updates: Create/update BR08/BR09/CD8 proof dossiers; record commutation/naturality target, failure modes, concrete finite instance traversal, and no-input-backflow audit.
Exit rule: The bridge is either proved as a theorem over derived objects, split into smaller prerequisite lemmas, or marked blocked. It may not feed earlier phases unless a separate backreaction/input handoff explicitly permits it.
